page: Microsoft Azure D32s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32s_v6 demonstrates strong performance in memory-intensive and processing tasks. Large-block memory bandwidth tests for both read and write operations place the instance in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 25%. Geekbench testing highlights strong single-core and multi-core capabilities, with image processing performance reaching the top 10% tier. For language model workloads, the instance achieves top 10% performance in text generation for small models (135M) and strong top 25% performance for medium models (7B), while maintaining average performance on larger 70B models.

A primary architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on remote Premium Storage options. This makes the instance highly dependent on network-attached storage performance. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high-density memory bandwidth provide a solid return on resource investment for general-purpose compute workloads without the overhead of specialized hardware. The instance is suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz, backed by 128.0 GB of RAM and a 260 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While the instance lacks local storage, requiring remote Premium Storage, it delivers strong benchmark results, placing in the top 25% for large-block memory bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and medium-model LLM inference. Image processing performance reaches the top 10% tier. This virtual machine is optimized for balanced workloads such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and advanced AMX instructions, delivering strong memory bandwidth and image processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and strong memory bandwidth for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing 4 GB of RAM per vCPU
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector operations
Delivers top 10% performance in image processing and small-model text generation
Lacks local storage, requiring remote Premium Storage configurations
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based inference
